title: Introducing IDEA – the International Development Economics Association
gist: Announcement of the International Development Economics Association (IDEA), founded January 2026 by 89 scholars from 40+ countries as the first global professional body for development economists, with bylaw-mandated geographic representation and a BREAD partnership. Thin promotional/announcement post with limited analytical signal.

insights.claim: The International Development Economics Association (IDEA) was founded in January 2026 by a Founding Board of 89 scholars from over 40 countries as the first global professional association of development economists.
insights.speaker: Owen Ozier
insights.so_what: Signals institutionalization and coordination of the development-economics field, which shapes research agendas that feed into World Bank and donor policy.
insights.quote: The International Development Economics Association (IDEA), founded in January 2026 by a Founding Board of 89 scholars from over 40 countries
insights.locator: Development economists now have a global professional home.

insights.claim: IDEA's bylaws require board representation from each of six world regions plus at-large seats, with particular attention to scholars in low- and middle-income countries.
insights.speaker: Owen Ozier
insights.so_what: Hard-coded geographic representation signals an intent to shift the field's center of gravity away from rich-country institutions.
insights.quote: Geographic representation is written into the bylaws, which require representatives employed in each of six world regions
insights.locator: IDEA is the first global professional association

insights.claim: IDEA approved a three-year partnership with the Bureau for Research and Economic Analysis of Development (BREAD) in May 2026, giving BREAD an ex-officio non-voting board seat.
insights.speaker: Owen Ozier
insights.so_what: Early alignment with established research networks suggests IDEA will consolidate rather than fragment the field's institutional landscape.
insights.quote: A three-year partnership with the Bureau for Research and Economic Analysis of Development (BREAD) was approved in May 2026
insights.locator: What about other organizations that already exist?

insights.claim: IDEA's planned activities include a working paper series, in-person and virtual conferences, career development and mentorship programs, and partnerships with existing field institutions.
insights.speaker: Owen Ozier
insights.so_what: A new working-paper channel and conference footprint are where field consensus forms — worth tracking for emerging development-policy themes.
insights.quote: IDEA's planned activities include a working paper series, conferences (in-person and virtual), career development and mentorship programs
